subject: Social Science - Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning and importance of agriculture
Recognize physical and human factors affecting agriculture
Identify major food crops and cash crops
Understand modern and sustainable agricultural practices

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Agriculture
student_activities: List agricultural activities seen in their village or town
Discuss why agriculture is important for people

lesson_plan.title: Types of Farming
student_activities: Create a comparison list of subsistence and commercial farming
Give examples of crops grown for each type

lesson_plan.title: Factors Affecting Agriculture
student_activities: List physical and human factors affecting farming
Discuss how rainfall affects crop growth

lesson_plan.title: Crop Seasons in India
student_activities: List crops grown in each season
Draw a simple seasonal farming chart

lesson_plan.title: Food Crops and Cash Crops
student_activities: Classify crops into food crops and cash crops

lesson_plan.title: Plantation Agriculture
student_activities: Locate tea and coffee producing regions on the map
Write a short paragraph on plantation farming

lesson_plan.title: Modern and Sustainable Agriculture (Revision)
student_activities: List modern tools used in agriculture

teaching_aids: Pictures of crops and farming tools
teacher_tip: Use real-life examples from local farming practices and encourage students to share experiences related to agriculture in their community to make learning more practical and engaging.
